destinations.evidence: Before execution, nodebuilder reads all executors from the configured TEE registry and updates the SPC verifier registry at the current block number, propagating read/update errors as block execution failures.
The registry refresh occurs at the start of block execution before transaction strategy setup and transaction execution.

equivalence_argument: Nodebuilder owns the execution wrapper; it refreshes the SPC verifier from on-chain TEE registry state before transaction execution and fails the block on registry read or update errors.
rationale: The new `RitualBlockExecutorProvider` wraps block execution with `update_executor_registry_from_block`, reads executors via `read_all_executors`, converts them to verifier registry tuples, calls `update_registry_from_data`, and propagates errors as `BlockExecutionError`. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Before executing a block, the Ritual block executor must read all executors from the configured TEE service registry contract and update the SPC verifier's in-memory registry at the current block number; registry read or update failures must fail block execution.
suggested_falsification_tests: Run `execute_one` with a mock database containing registry executor entries and a mock SPC verifier, assert `update_registry_from_data` is called before any transaction execution with the expected executor tuples and block number, and assert injected registry read/update errors cause `execute_one` to return an error.
summary: Nodebuilder refreshes the SPC verifier registry from the on-chain TEE registry before block execution.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Inject registry read/update failures and assert execute_one returns an error before executing transactions; with valid entries, assert update_registry_from_data receives the expected tuples and block number.
title: Block execution refreshes SPC registry from on-chain TEE registry
